page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server configured with 288 vCPUs across 144 physical Intel Xeon 6985P-C cores. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture system features a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations. The server is equipped with 2232 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing approximately 7.75 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of six local NVMe SSDs totaling 16242 GB of capacity. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, achieving top-tier status in both small-block cached and large-block uncached read operations. Database and key-value store performance is exceptionally strong, as demonstrated by top-tier results in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations, which reached 422.06 million operations per second. While single-threaded compression and decompression benchmarks are in the top 10%, multi-core Geekbench scores align with the middle 50%, and text generation for small LLM models is relatively weak.

Given its bare-metal architecture, high memory density, and massive local NVMe storage, this server offers a specialized hardware profile designed for resource-intensive enterprise applications. The qualitative cost efficiency is optimized for workloads that require direct hardware access, high memory capacity, and low-latency local storage without virtualization overhead. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory data stores like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and complex data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 288 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, paired with 2232 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory and 16242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Lacking a GPU, this x86_64 system relies on its high core count and advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX to deliver top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance. Benchmark data highlights exceptional capabilities in database operations and Redis key-value transactions, alongside strong single-core processing. This high-density memory and storage profile represents a specialized hardware class optimized for demanding enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive processing tasks requiring direct physical hardware access.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2232 GB DDR5 RAM, and 16242 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 vCPUs, 2232 GB DDR5 RAM, and 16242 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-core bare-metal platform combining massive DDR5 memory capacity with high-speed local NVMe storage for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 288 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz
Equipped with 2232 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Includes 16242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across six drives
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for large-scale databases and in-memory data stores
